content.problemStatement:
Complete the code for Binary Search to find the index of an element KKK in the given array AAA.
Output the index of the element of KKK, if KKK is present in AAA, else output −1-1−1.
constraints: Array elements are distinct and sorted in ascending order
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers n and k, where n is the size of the sorted array and k is the target value. The second line contains n space‑separated distinct integers in ascending order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the zero‑based index of k in the array, or -1 if k does not appear.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. In the array, the value 7 is at zero‑based index 3, so the program prints 3.
The target 2 is not present in the array, therefore the output is -1.
The first element equals the target, its index is 0.

solution.approach: For each test case we perform a classic binary search on the sorted array. We keep two pointers, left and right, initially at the bounds of the array. While left <= right we compute middle = left + (right - left) / 2. If arr[middle] equals k we return middle. If arr[middle] > k we move the right bound to middle‑1, otherwise we move the left bound to middle+1. If the loop finishes without finding k we return -1. This runs in O(log n) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * log n) – each test case uses binary search which is logarithmic in the array size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input array.
